title: senecio sterquilinus
intro: Senecio sterquilinus, also known as the dungwort, is a flowering plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The dungwort has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and its health benefits also extend to its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Senecio sterquilinus are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
